title: Paraboea parviflora
taxonomy: Paraboea parviflora is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. The Gesneriaceae family is part of the larger order Lamiales. The order Lamiales is a group of flowering plants that also includes mint and snapdragons. The classification of Paraboea parviflora reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this group of plants.

intro: Paraboea parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly found in tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Paraboea parviflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Paraboea parviflora is often used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are valued for their therapeutic potential in traditional medicine.
